content.text:
<think>
Looking at the ternary plot:

- **D.C.** is a teal dot positioned very far to the bottom-left (near the Democrat vertex), indicating it has an extremely high Democrat proportion (~92%).

- The **Democrat proportion axis** (blue diagonal lines) shows that points further toward the lower-left have higher Democrat proportions.

- Among the **red dots (Republican winners)**, I need to find which one is positioned furthest toward the lower-left (highest Democrat proportion, i.e., closest to D.C.'s Democrat proportion).

Looking at the red dots:
- **TX** is clearly positioned lower and more separated from the main red cluster, indicating it has less Republican proportion and a relatively higher Democrat proportion compared to other Republican-winning states.
- States like WY, WV, ND, TN, AR, OK, AK are all higher in the plot (more Republican, less Democrat).

TX (Texas) is the red dot sitting below the main Republican cluster. In the 2020 election, Texas had ~46.5% Democrat votes — the highest among all Republican-winning states. By contrast, Wyoming had ~27%, WV ~30%, etc.

D.C. had ~92% Democrat. The difference for Texas (~46%) is roughly 45.5 percentage points, smaller than any other Republican-winning state.
</think>

<answer>
Texas (TX)

Texas is the Republican-winning state that shows the least difference in Democrat vote proportion compared to D.C. In the ternary plot, TX is clearly positioned furthest toward the Democrat vertex among all red (Republican-winner) dots — sitting lower and more separated from the main red cluster. This corresponds to Texas having the highest Democrat proportion (~46.5%) among all Republican-winning states in 2020, compared to D.C.'s ~92% Democrat proportion — a difference of roughly 45 percentage points, smaller than any other Republican-winning state.
</answer>
